texts.full_text: droplets impinging on BN arrays with different wetting properties is distinct. In the case of superhydrophobic films, the water droplet bounces off the surface several times whereas in less hydrophobic films it does not rebound and remains pinned to the surface. These results provide a facile route for the selective preparation of hierarchical BN nanostructure array films

texts.full_text: Rome, the Secchi disk has been an invaluable tool for scientists studying aquatic environments worldwide. The technique is simple yet effective: a white disc (usually 20 cm or8 inches in diameter) with alternating black and white quadrants is lowered into the water until it disappears from sight when viewed vertically from above. The depth at which this occurs, known as Secchi disk depth (SDD), serves as an estimate of water clarity. It's influenced by factors like sunlight penetration, suspended solids or colored dissolved organic matter in the water column. For phytoplankton mapping purposes, lower SDDs often indicate higher concentrations of these microscopic plants due to their light-absorbing properties. The app you mentioned likelyallows users to input coordinates and Secchi disk depth measurements taken from boats at sea. This crowdsourced data can then be aggregated on a global scale to create maps showing phytoplankton abundance across the world's oceans, providing valuable insights into marine ecosystems' health and dynamics. For instance, researchers might track changes in phytoplankton populations over time or investigate how they respond to environmental factors like temperature fluctuations, nutrient availability, or ocean currents. By understanding these patterns better, scientists can improve predictions about phenomena such as harmful algal blooms (HABs), which pose threats to marine life andhuman health. So far, the response has been encouraging with many sailors contributing data from various regions around the globe. However, there's still a need for more readings from underrepresented areas like the southern hemisphere ocean

texts.full_text: learned that the late security guard was a fan of "Shark Tank" and immediately offered assistance to the family. “I want to help with any expenses you might have,” John said, emphasizing his support. Salter III expressed gratitude and mentioned his commitment to taking care of his mother during this difficult time. The conversation turned emotional as both men discussed the impact of the loss. “I could hear it in her voice.” Salter III said John told him he could reach out any time his family needed something. NEW YORK, NEW YORK – MAY 16: Daymond John attends the 26th Annual Webby Awards on May 16, 2022 in New Yor
